summary: Aventra Estates – a modern, premium real estate website and mobile app (similar to 99acres) with white/gold/black luxury UI. Brand tagline: 'Your Trusted Property Partner'. Built with React/Next.js frontend, Node.js backend, MongoDB database, cloud hosting. Targets Pune localities (Kondhwa, Undri, NIBM, Wanowrie) with plans for India-wide scalability.
architectural_notes: Stack: React/Next.js frontend, Node.js backend, MongoDB database, cloud hosting
Premium UI theme: white + gold + black color palette with modern animations
Scalable architecture for future India-wide expansion

existing_features.title: Apply a luxury premium visual theme throughout the entire application using a white, gold (#C9A84C / #D4AF37), and black color palette. Typography should use elegant serif/sans-serif pairings. Include smooth scroll animations, fade-in transitions on section entry, and hover micro-interactions on cards and buttons. The overall aesthetic must match top-tier real estate platforms with a clean, spacious layout.

Build a Homepage with: (1) a full-viewport hero section featuring a looping background property video with the brand name 'Aventra Estates', tagline 'Your Trusted Property Partner', and a prominent 'Post Property Free' CTA button; (2) a search bar overlaid on the hero with tab filters for Buy / Rent / Sell, a location dropdown pre-populated with Pune localities (Kondhwa, Undri, NIBM, Wanowrie, Baner, Kothrud), a budget range selector, and a property type selector (1BHK, 2BHK, 3BHK, Villa, Plot, Commercial); (3) a Featured Properties horizontal scroll section; (4) an Under Construction Projects section; (5) a Luxury Collection showcase section; (6) a Customer Testimonials carousel section.

Build a Property Listing Page that displays properties in both grid and list view (togglable). Each property card must show: a property image, price, location, carpet area and built-up area, amenity icons (Gym, Pool, Parking, Garden), a 'Contact Agent' button, and a 'WhatsApp' button that opens a WhatsApp chat link. Include sidebar filters for price range, property type, BHK type, and location.

Build a Property Detail Page with: an image gallery slider at the top; a video tour embed section; a full text description; a static Google Maps placeholder section (displaying locality map using an embedded iframe or static map image); an EMI Calculator widget (inputs: loan amount, interest rate, tenure — outputs monthly EMI); a Loan Assistance section with CTA; agent details card with profile photo, name, and contact options; and an Enquiry Form (name, phone, email, message fields).

Build a User Panel with: a Login/Register page (email + password authentication); a user Dashboard showing the user's posted listings and received leads/enquiries; a Post Property form (multi-step: basic info → location → details → upload images → preview & submit); the ability to edit or delete own listings; and a Saved Properties / Wishlist page.

Build an Admin Panel accessible only to admin-role users with: a listings management table (approve, reject, feature, delete listings); a user management table (view, deactivate users); a featured property toggle control; and a simple analytics dashboard showing total properties, total users, pending approvals, and total enquiries as stat cards.

Ensure the entire application is fully mobile-responsive. All pages — homepage, listing page, detail page, user panel, admin panel — must render correctly and be usable on screens from 320px to 1440px width. Navigation should collapse into a mobile hamburger menu on small screens.

Generate and integrate a premium hero banner image and a logo mark for Aventra Estates. The logo should feature a stylized architectural icon or abstract estate emblem in gold on a black or white background. The hero banner should depict a luxury modern residential property exterior at golden hour, suitable as a fallback when the hero video is not available.

Add a dedicated 'Rent' property category throughout the application. The Search Bar on the homepage already has a 'Rent' tab — ensure it correctly filters and navigates to the property listing page showing only rental listings. Add 'For Rent' as a valid listing type/status in the backend data model alongside existing statuses. Allow users to post properties listed as 'For Rent' via the Post Property multi-step form by including a 'Listing Type' field (Buy / Rent). Display a 'For Rent' badge on property cards and detail pages for rental listings.

Update all site-wide contact information to reflect the official Aventra Estates details: phone numbers +917020271267 and +919535511171, email aventraestate@gmail.com, and office address 'Near Goodluck Apartment, Kondhwa, Pune 411048'. This information must appear in the Footer component and on any Contact / About section. WhatsApp quick-contact links should use +917020271267 as the default number.

Update the multi-step Post Property form (PostPropertyPage) to include input fields for: multiple photo URL entries or an image upload list, a 'Has Balcony' toggle/checkbox, a 'Car Parking Spots' number input, and a multi-select or checkbox list for amenities (Gym, Pool, Parking, Garden, Balcony, Lift, Security, Clubhouse, Power Backup). These fields should appear in the appropriate step of the multi-step form and be submitted with the property data. Also update the EditPropertyPage to include the same new fields, pre-populated from existing property data.

Update PropertyCard and PropertyListingPage to display the new property fields: show up to 4 property photos in the card image area (at least the primary/first photo), and show amenity icons for all relevant amenities (Gym, Pool, Parking, Garden, Balcony, Lift, etc.) present on the property. Display a 'Balcony' icon/badge and a 'Car Parking: N' indicator on each property card where applicable.

Update the Property Detail Page (PropertyDetailPage) to display the full photo gallery using the photos array in the ImageGallerySlider. Show all amenities as a visually styled grid of icon+label pairs. Display balcony availability and number of car parking spots in the property features section.

Update the Admin Listings Page (AdminListingsPage) to clearly show the approval workflow: display a 'Pending Approval' tab or filter to surface newly submitted properties awaiting review. Each pending property row must have 'Approve' and 'Reject' action buttons that call the updatePropertyStatus backend method. Display the current status (Pending, Active, Rejected) as a color-coded badge on each row.

Update the User Dashboard (UserDashboard) to show the approval status of each of the user's submitted properties. Display a status badge (Pending Approval, Active, Rejected) next to each listing so the user knows whether their property is live or awaiting review.

In PostPropertyPage (Features & Photos step), remove all photo URL text input fields and replace them with a file upload input that accepts JPEG image files (accept='image/jpeg,image/jpg'). Allow uploading multiple files. Read uploaded files as binary data (e.g., via FileReader as ArrayBuffer or base64) and store them in component state for submission. Display thumbnail previews of selected images before submission.

In EditPropertyPage, remove all photo URL text input fields and replace them with a JPEG file upload input (accept='image/jpeg,image/jpg'). Pre-populate the photo preview area with existing photos from the property data. Allow the user to upload new JPEG files to replace or add to existing photos. Display thumbnail previews of both existing and newly selected photos.

Update PropertyCard, PropertyListingPage, PropertyDetailPage (ImageGallerySlider), and any other component that renders property photos to handle the new blob/base64 photo format. Convert stored photo blob data to object URLs or base64 data URIs for rendering in tags. Ensure backward compatibility where no photos exist (show a placeholder image).

new_feature_requests.summary: Homepage: hero video, search filters (Buy/Sell/Rent, location, budget, property type), featured properties, under construction projects, luxury collection, testimonials, Post Property Free CTA
Property Listing Page: grid/list view, property cards with image, price, location, area, amenities icons, Contact Agent and WhatsApp buttons
Property Detail Page: image gallery slider, video tour, full description, Google Map integration, EMI calculator, loan assistance section, agent profile, enquiry form
User Panel: login/register, dashboard, post property, upload multiple images & videos, track leads, edit/delete listing
Admin Panel: approve listings, manage users, featured property control, analytics dashboard
Mobile App Features: Android & iOS friendly, push notifications, save/wishlist property, direct call & WhatsApp integration, in-app chat system
SEO optimization: fast loading, mobile responsive, location-based keyword pages

Implement backend data models and API endpoints for: Properties (title, description, price, location, property type, BHK type, carpet area, built-up area, amenities, images, status: active/pending/sold, isFeatured, isLuxury, isUnderConstruction); Users (name, email, phone, role: user/admin, wishlist); Enquiries (propertyId, senderName, senderPhone, senderEmail, message, timestamp); Testimonials (author, quote, rating). Provide CRUD operations for all entities.
Add contact information to the site: phone numbers +917020271267 and +919535511171, email aventraestate@gmail.com, office address near Goodluck Apartment, Kondhwa, Pune 411048
Add Rent Property section/listing support so users can browse and post rental properties
Add photos, balcony, car parking, and amenities details to property listings and cards
Property submission approval workflow: submitted properties require admin approval before going live

Extend the backend Property data model to include: a 'photos' field (array of image URLs), a 'hasBalcony' boolean field, a 'carParking' field (number of car parking spots), and an expanded 'amenities' field that supports a structured list including Gym, Pool, Parking, Garden, Balcony, Lift, Security, Clubhouse, and Power Backup. Update all relevant backend CRUD methods (createProperty, updateProperty, getProperties, getPropertyById) to handle these new fields.

Implement a property submission approval workflow in the backend. When a user submits a new property via createProperty, its status must default to 'pending' (awaiting admin review). Add or confirm an admin-only updatePropertyStatus method that allows admins to approve (set status to 'active') or reject (set status to 'rejected') a pending property. Only properties with 'active' status should appear in public-facing queries (getProperties, getFeaturedProperties, etc.).
Replace photo URL input fields with actual JPEG/image file upload inputs for property photos

Update the backend Property data model to store photos as base64-encoded JPEG blobs (or stable binary blobs) instead of URL strings. Change the 'photos' field type from [Text] to [Blob] or a record containing filename and blob data. Update createProperty, updateProperty, and getPropertyById to accept and return photo blob data accordingly.
